_comment: HUMAN-OWNED. Hand-edit this; the ORCID sync reads it and never overwrites it. Use it to drop works you don't want in the public bibliography. Add a `reason` so future-you knows why.
_howToUse: excludeTypes: drop whole ORCID work types. Your record currently contains:
e.g. set ["conference-paper", "other"] to keep only papers + thesis + preprints.
exclude: drop individual works. Match on doi (preferred), bibcode, or exact title.
Run `pnpm sync:pubs` after editing to regenerate.
_policy: Conference proceedings are excluded BY TYPE, not one-by-one: in astronomy they
carry little weight and Anna does not want them in the bibliography. A type rule
means future deposits drop automatically instead of silently reappearing.
The Ph.D. thesis is excluded here because it is presented under Education on /cv.

exclude.reason: Ph.D. thesis - presented under Education on /cv instead of the paper list.

IAU proceedings (Highlights of Astronomy), which ORCID mistypes as a journal-article. A conference-summary Anna neither wrote nor reviewed — every attendee was listed as an author — so it does not belong in the bibliography. Proceedings are excluded per policy above.
